CREATE TABLE test_table (id UInt32, value Float64) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
ALTER TABLE test_table ADD STATISTICS (value) TYPE (avg, count) IF NOT EXISTS;
ALTER TABLE test_table ADD STATISTICS (value) TYPE (min, max) IF NOT EXISTS;
ALTER TABLE test_table MODIFY STATISTICS (value) TYPE (sum) IF EXISTS;
ALTER TABLE test_table DROP STATISTICS (value) IF EXISTS;
ALTER TABLE test_table CLEAR STATISTICS (value) IF EXISTS;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE value > 100) LIMIT 10;
ALTER TABLE test_table MATERIALIZE STATISTICS (value) IF EXISTS;
CREATE TABLE test_table_2 (id UInt32, description String) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
ALTER TABLE test_table_2 ADD STATISTICS (description) TYPE (unique) IF NOT EXISTS;
ALTER TABLE test_table_2 MODIFY STATISTICS (description) TYPE (length) IF EXISTS;
ALTER TABLE test_table_2 CLEAR STATISTICS (description) IF EXISTS;
SELECT a.id, b.description FROM test_table AS a JOIN test_table_2 AS b ON a.id = b.id;
ALTER TABLE test_table_2 DROP STATISTICS (description) IF EXISTS;
CREATE TABLE test_table_3 (id UInt32, value Float64, created_at DateTime) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY created_at;
ALTER TABLE test_table_3 ADD STATISTICS (value, created_at) TYPE (avg, count, min) IF NOT EXISTS;
ALTER TABLE test_table_3 MATERIALIZE STATISTICS (ALL) IF EXISTS;
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, 150.5), (2, 99.3), (3, 250.5);
INSERT INTO test_table_2 VALUES (1, 'Test A'), (2, 'Test B');
